citation: Emir Kamenica and Matthew Gentzkow (2011), Bayesian Persuasion, American Economic Review 101(6):2590-2615, doi:10.1257/aer.101.6.2590
copyright_policy: Only citation, functional analysis, and a remote-byte hash are stored. No source prose is retained or exposed to writers.
derived_function: The paper motivates a precise persuasion problem, introduces the operative information-design choice, and connects characterization results to recognizable applications; the transferable lesson is functional sequencing, not wording or voice.
